metadata.backbone_note: Metadata label only; this structural runner does not call an LLM reader.

benchmark.description: Three-step temporal supersession with current, history, and provenance checks.

events.text: Alice lives in Seoul as of January 2024.
events.subject: Alice

events.text: Alice moved to Rome in June 2025.
events.subject: Alice

events.text: Alice moved to Dublin in February 2026.
events.subject: Alice

questions.prompt: Where does Alice live now after the latest move?
questions.subject: Alice

questions.prompt: What is Alice's full residence timeline?
questions.subject: Alice

questions.prompt: Which exact source supports Alice's current residence?
questions.subject: Alice

questions.prompt: Where did Alice live in December 2025?
questions.subject: Alice

benchmark.description: After rollback, the retracted bad source must no longer justify current truth.

events.text: Verified residency file says RollbackSource1 lives in Madrid.
events.subject: RollbackSource1

events.text: Bad bulk import says RollbackSource1 lives in Lima.

benchmark.description: The correct provenance source differs between main and a hypothetical branch.

events.text: Main employment contract says BranchSource2 works at Lab C.
events.subject: BranchSource2

events.text: Grant planning branch says BranchSource2 would work at Lab F.
events.subject: BranchSource2

questions.prompt: On main, which source supports where BranchSource2 works?
questions.subject: BranchSource2

questions.prompt: On grant-branch-2, which source supports where BranchSource2 works?
questions.subject: BranchSource2

benchmark.description: A rolled-back same-object corroborating source must not remain the current justification.

events.text: Verified municipal file says RollbackSameObject3 lives in Lima.
events.subject: RollbackSameObject3

events.text: Corrupted mirror also says RollbackSameObject3 lives in Lima.
events.subject: RollbackSameObject3

events.text: Rollback corrupted corroborating source for RollbackSameObject3.

questions.prompt: After rollback, which source currently justifies RollbackSameObject3's residence?
questions.subject: RollbackSameObject3

benchmark.description: The same object is true on main and branch, but each context has a different governing source.

events.text: Main personnel file says SameObjectBranch4 works at Lab F.
events.subject: SameObjectBranch4

events.text: Audit branch independently confirms SameObjectBranch4 works at Lab F.
events.subject: SameObjectBranch4

questions.prompt: On main, which source currently justifies where SameObjectBranch4 works?
questions.subject: SameObjectBranch4

questions.prompt: On audit-branch-4, which source currently justifies where SameObjectBranch4 works?
questions.subject: SameObjectBranch4

benchmark.description: After a winning merge and a weaker conflicting merge, the current justification should remain the winning branch source.

events.text: Old HR file says MergeSource5 works at Lab F.

Rumor branch says MergeSource5 works at Lab D.
events.subject: MergeSource5

events.text: Merge rumor-reorg-source-5 into main for review.

questions.prompt: After both merges, which source currently justifies where MergeSource5 works?
questions.subject: MergeSource5

benchmark.description: Lower-trust branch merge should preserve an unresolved conflict for manual review.

benchmark.description: Concurrent main and branch updates should surface an unresolved merge conflict.

events.text: Baseline HR file says ConcurrentMerge1 works at Lab B.

events.subject: ConcurrentMerge1

questions.prompt: After concurrent main and branch updates, should ConcurrentMerge1's merge be unresolved?
questions.subject: ConcurrentMerge1

benchmark.description: Two competing branch merges should leave the lower-trust conflict unresolved instead of silently overwriting.

events.text: Initial HR file says CompetingBranches2 works at Lab C.

Merge draft-reorg-2 into main for conflict review.

questions.prompt: After merging two competing branches, should CompetingBranches2's workplace remain under manual review?
questions.subject: CompetingBranches2

benchmark.description: A merge with non-overlapping temporal windows should coexist by time slice rather than overwrite.
